headline: Nvidia CEO sees robotics as next major sector in South Korea
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said on Friday that ‌he sees robotics as the next ‌major sector in South Korea. headline: Quantinuum Stock Falls After Uninspiring Quantum IPO Debut. What Went Wrong?
summary: Quantinuum  stock popped in its trading debut, opening 13% above its initial public offering price in what seemed to be a major win for the quantum computing industry.  Regardless, the stock outperformed other notable names in the rest sector:  IonQ  closed down 3.8% while  D-Wave Quantum  and  Rigetti Computing  added 0.3% each.  The stock looked set to fall on its second day of trading, pointing 3.6% lower at $58.18 in premarket trading Friday.

content.title: Quantinuum Stock Falls After Uninspiring Quantum IPO Debut. What Went Wrong?
content.summary: Quantinuum  stock popped in its trading debut, opening 13% above its initial public offering price in what seemed to be a major win for the quantum computing industry.  Regardless, the stock outperformed other notable names in the rest sector:  IonQ  closed down 3.8% while  D-Wave Quantum  and  Rigetti Computing  added 0.3% each.  The stock looked set to fall on its second day of trading, pointing 3.6% lower at $58.18 in premarket trading Friday.

headline: Nvidia CEO says robotics is South Korea's next big sector, points to 'some suprises'
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on Friday said robotics will be South Korea's next major sector, as he arrived for his second visit in seven months, ‌underscoring deepening ties with local firms in not only chips but also robotics and AI factories.  "Because Korea is ‌a manufacturing centre of the world, we can apply the robotics technology, the physical AI technology that we invent here for the industry," he told ​reporters after landing at Gimpo International Airport on a flight from Taiwan.  South Korea is a key Asian manufacturing powerhouse - home to major manufacturers of chips, electronics, cars and ships.

content.title: Nvidia CEO says robotics is South Korea's next big sector, points to 'some suprises'
content.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on Friday said robotics will be South Korea's next major sector, as he arrived for his second visit in seven months, ‌underscoring deepening ties with local firms in not only chips but also robotics and AI factories.  "Because Korea is ‌a manufacturing centre of the world, we can apply the robotics technology, the physical AI technology that we invent here for the industry," he told ​reporters after landing at Gimpo International Airport on a flight from Taiwan.  South Korea is a key Asian manufacturing powerhouse - home to major manufacturers of chips, electronics, cars and ships.
